text.title: Cloud-native Backup with AWS Backup and S3 Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Cloud-native Backup with AWS Backup and S3` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: S3 backup buckets MUST enable versioning, block all public access, server-side KMS encryption, and ≥ 1 lifecycle rule transitioning objects after 30 days.
rule.rationale: These four settings are the minimum viable backup-bucket configuration.

rule.statement: Buckets containing critical backups MUST enable Object Lock in GOVERNANCE or COMPLIANCE mode with ≥ 30-day retention.
rule.rationale: Immutable lock is the only protection against ransomware deletes of backup data.

rule.statement: The daily-runner IAM role MUST NOT include `s3:DeleteObject`, `s3:DeleteObjectVersion`, or `s3:PutBucketObjectLockConfiguration`. A separate break-glass account holds those permissions.
rule.rationale: Air-gapping delete permission prevents a compromised runner from destroying backups.

rule.statement: AWS Backup plans MUST include a cross-region copy rule for production data.
rule.rationale: Single-region backups die with the region.

rule.statement: Backup vaults MUST use a customer-managed KMS key (CMK), not the AWS-managed alias.
rule.rationale: CMK rotation + access control are the audit trail; aws/backup alias lacks per-vault granularity.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
